seo.title: API-First Products implementation checklist
seo.description: A practical checklist for shipping API-First Products projects safely.

content.intro: This checklist outlines the technical requirements for transitioning an API-first product from staging to a production environment, ensuring reliability, security, and developer satisfaction.

items.title: OpenAPI Spec Validation
items.description: Verify that the OpenAPI 3.0/3.1 specification matches the actual implementation using a tool like Prism or Dredd to prevent drift.

items.title: Standardized Error Objects
items.description: Ensure all error responses follow the RFC 7807 (Problem Details for HTTP APIs) format, including specific error codes and human-readable messages.

items.title: Idempotency Implementation
items.description: Implement 'Idempotency-Key' header support for all POST and PATCH requests to prevent duplicate processing during network retries.

items.title: Cursor-Based Pagination
items.description: Replace offset/limit pagination with cursor-based pagination for all collection endpoints to ensure consistent performance and data integrity.

items.title: Naming Consistency
items.description: Audit all endpoints to ensure consistent casing (e.g., camelCase or snake_case) and pluralization across the entire resource tree.

items.title: API Key Hashing
items.description: Verify that API keys are stored using a cryptographically secure hash (like SHA-256) and are never logged or stored in plain text.

items.title: Rate Limiting by Tier
items.description: Configure global and per-user rate limits at the gateway level (e.g., using Zuplo or Cloudflare) to prevent DoS and manage resource allocation.

items.title: CORS Policy Restriction
items.description: Restrict Cross-Origin Resource Sharing (CORS) to specific allowed domains rather than using wildcards in production.

items.title: JWT Signature Verification
items.description: If using JWTs, ensure the backend validates signatures, expiration times, and 'aud'/'iss' claims on every request.

items.title: TLS 1.3 Enforcement
items.description: Disable support for TLS 1.0 and 1.1; enforce TLS 1.2 or 1.3 for all incoming API traffic.

items.title: Usage Event Reporting
items.description: Test the integration between the API gateway and the billing engine (e.g., Stripe Billing) to ensure usage events are reported within a <5 minute lag.

items.title: Quota Enforcement
items.description: Verify that the system returns a 429 Too Many Requests status code immediately when a user exceeds their plan's monthly or daily quota.

items.title: Webhook Signature Validation
items.description: Implement signature verification for incoming webhooks from payment providers to prevent spoofing of subscription updates.

items.title: Grace Period Logic
items.description: Programmatically handle 'past_due' subscription statuses to allow a 3-day grace period before revoking API access.

items.title: Billing Metadata Injection
items.description: Ensure every API request is tagged with an internal 'organization_id' derived from the API key to facilitate accurate cost-per-customer analysis.

items.title: Structured Logging
items.description: Implement JSON-based structured logging for all requests, including request IDs, response times, and status codes.

items.title: Distributed Tracing
items.description: Integrate OpenTelemetry to trace requests across microservices, specifically monitoring latency in AI inference or database calls.

items.title: Health Check Endpoints
items.description: Deploy a /health or /ready endpoint that checks downstream dependencies (DB, Redis, LLM providers) and returns a 503 if any are unreachable.

items.title: Timeout Configuration
items.description: Set explicit timeouts for all external service calls (e.g., 30s for AI wrappers) to prevent hanging worker processes.

items.title: Circuit Breakers
items.description: Implement circuit breakers on non-critical downstream services to allow the API to fail gracefully during partial outages.

items.title: Automatic SDK Generation
items.description: Set up a CI/CD pipeline to generate and publish TypeScript and Python client libraries whenever the OpenAPI spec is updated.

items.title: Interactive Documentation
items.description: Deploy a hosted documentation portal (e.g., Mintlify or Stoplight) that allows developers to execute test requests directly from the browser.

items.title: Sandbox Environment
items.description: Provide a separate 'test' API key mode that interacts with a non-production database for developer integration testing.

items.title: API Versioning Header
items.description: Enforce versioning via a custom header (e.g., 'X-API-Version') or URL path to ensure backward compatibility for existing users.

items.title: Public Status Page
items.description: Launch a status page (e.g., Better Stack or Statuspage.io) that reflects real-time uptime and latency of the API endpoints.
